steps.text: Determine if the licensee’s procedures for radiation protection at the facility meet regulatory requirements, and licensee commitments.
Determine if Form NRC-3, "Notice to Employees," is posted as required.
Determine if restricted areas are posted and controlled as required.
Determine if appropriate protective clothing is used by facility personnel as required.
Determine if the licensee’s personnel dosimetry program is effective, maintained and meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if licensee personnel, equipment and materials exiting a controlled area are monitored as required.
Determine if personnel working with or around radioactive materials have been instructed in radiation protection as required.
Determine if required radiation surveys, sampling and monitoring were performed in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the licensee has implemented a calibration program for the survey, sampling and monitoring instruments in use as required.
Determine if exposure limits in 10 CFR Part 20 were satisfied.
Determine if changes with the potential to affect radiation protection activities were consistent with 10
Determine if exposure limits in 10 CFR Part 20 were satisfied.
Determine if changes with the potential to affect radiation protection activities were consistent with 10 CFR 50.59, the license and Technical Specification (TS) requirements.
Determine if the licensee addressed radiologically significant events in accordance with regulatory and the licensee’s procedural requirements.
Determine if an ALARA program has been implemented as required.
Determine if the licensee has developed, documented and implemented a radiation protection program in accordance with regulatory requirements.
Determine if the respiratory protection program meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if planned special exposures were made in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the dose to the embryo/fetus for declared pregnant women met regulatory requirements.
Inform appropriate management to determine the course of action.

preconditions.text: The inspection is for radiological controls at Class I Research and Test reactors (not NRC material licenses or Agreement State licenses).
There are concerns relative to other license activities or conditions.

constraints.text: If practicable, use direct observation to verify inspection requirements. Otherwise use some combination of records review, independent measurements, or discussions or walk-throughs with involved licensee personnel.

steps.text: Use some combination of records review, independent measurements, or discussions or walk-throughs with involved licensee personnel to verify inspection requirements.
Determine if a potential or actual hazard exists.
Initiate appropriate regulatory action.

preconditions.text: Consult with responsible management and the NRR project manager.

constraints.text: Inspection requirements in this inspection procedure are not to be repeated if they are fulfilled in other inspection procedures or vice versa.

References for this guidance are to aid in the technical evaluation of licensee programs and are not to be used as requirements unless the licensee has committed to the specific guidance document in writing.

steps.text: Review the changes to the facility's radiation protection procedures.
Verify Form NRC-3 is conspicuously posted.
Ask the licensee where Form NRC-3 forms are posted.
Directly observe the Form NRC-3 postings in one or two areas.
Observe the controls for two radiologically controlled areas.
Verify the area is posted and controlled for airborne contamination.

preconditions.text: Postings of Form NRC-3 are not readily apparent.
Volatile radioactive materials are used in an area.

constraints.text: Form NRC-3 should be posted in sufficient quantities and locations to permit workers to observe them while traveling to or from any location to which the notice applies.
Radiologically controlled areas are required to be conspicuously posted.

steps.text: Evaluate the licensee’s use of controls for radiation areas unique to Research and Test reactors, such as pneumatic irradiation sample transfer systems for 'rabbits,' beam ports, and other potential radiation work areas for experimental programs.
Evaluate the licensee’s use of shielding and controls used to remove or modify shielding.
Observe the protective clothing worn by two facility experimenters or other respective personnel during their work activities.
Conduct discussions with two individuals to determine their use of protective clothing.

preconditions.text: Observation of protective clothing is possible.
Observation of protective clothing is not practical.

constraints.text: Do not enter a high radiation area without a licensee escort trained in radiation protection who is knowledgeable of the high radiation area and the health physics precautions to be taken.

steps.text: Check alarming dosimeters (chirpers) to assure that they are operational.
Observe two selected facility workers leaving the applicable controlled area to determine if the dosimeter readings are actually recorded.
Review the process of film or TLD badge processing.
Observe four facility workers leaving a controlled area.
Check all personnel, tools, equipment, and protective clothing for contamination before leaving a controlled area.
Monitor hands before the probe is removed from its mounting.

preconditions.text: If alarming dosimeters (chirpers) are used
If licensee procedures require that personnel exposures on direct reading dosimeters be recorded
If the licensee processes its own film or TLD badges
Where hand held friskers are used

constraints.text: Dosimetry must be worn as required.
Assess the need for extremity monitors for handling activated samples, and activated or contaminated experimental equipment.
Dosimetry processing should meet the requirements of 10 CFR 20.1501 for NVLAP accreditation.
The exit monitor must be shielded from extraneous radiation sources that would mask contamination.

steps.text: Monitor hands before removing the probe from its mounting.
Remove the probe from its mounting.
Detect alpha contamination using a hand held gas flow proportional counter with a thin mylar window or by smear survey and analyses using a 2 or 4 pi gas flow proportional counter.
Perform nasal smears.
Observe respective activities of two permanent facility workers and, where applicable, two experimenters or students.
Discuss the radiation safety training received by these individuals to confirm that appropriate training was actually received and understood.
Observe two radiological surveys, sampling, or monitoring activities, other than exit surveys, by accompanying personnel.

preconditions.text: Hand held friskers are used.
Alpha contamination is suspected.
Personnel contamination by alpha emitters is suspected.

constraints.text: Prevent contamination of the probe.

steps.text: Accompany a person qualified in the licensee’s radiation control techniques on normal rounds, or request to be shown how tasks are normally accomplished, to observe two radiological surveys, sampling, or monitoring activities.
Determine whether personnel conducting these activities are qualified by training and experience through direct observation and discussion.

Verify whether technicians take smears or instrument readings, as appropriate, in areas readily accessible to facility personnel, paying particular attention to beam ports, experimental stations, and ion exchangers.
Examine the licensee’s training records to confirm that technicians are sufficiently trained.
Verify if surveys were performed as required by the licensee's procedures and if exposure rates are consistent with the ALARA program.
Determine whether radiation levels are above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20.

preconditions.text: If concerns arise regarding technician qualifications
If radiation levels above background are suspected in areas outside of the restricted area
Operability checks (e.g., source and battery checks) must be performed on all instrumentation before use

constraints.text: All instrumentation used during these activities are required to be operable, calibrated, and maintained as required by 10 CFR 20.1501(b) and the licensee’s procedures.

steps.text: Determine whether radiation levels are above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D.
Determine if the licensee evaluated personnel occupancy of these areas and the potential dose to occupants.
Verify if the licensee checks for excessive levels of contamination within restricted areas and at the exits from the restricted area.
Perform independent radiological surveys as needed to verify licensee assumptions or measurements.
Compare inspector survey measurements with those completed by the licensee in the same areas.
Review surveys conducted by the licensee during both shutdown and operations following changes in shielding.
Verify if there is reasonable agreement with the licensee's records.

preconditions.text: Radiation levels above background are suspected in areas outside of the restricted area.
Radiation levels above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D are detected.
Independent surveys are performed by the inspector.

constraints.text: Good housekeeping practices generally suggest a barrier any time that contamination levels exceed double the background unless the contamination is removable and the area is immediately decontaminated.

steps.text: Determine how the licensee assures that levels are within the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR Part 20, Subparts C and D.
Examine surveys of solid waste material to determine whether the licensee complies with their procedures and requirements.
Verify the calibration of radiation monitoring instrument(s) used in the licensee’s radiation surveys.
Examine the technical adequacy of calibration procedures.
Review 50 percent of the facility’s personnel dosimetry record.

preconditions.text: The potential exists for airborne contamination
The facility performs its own calibrations

constraints.text: Records for survey, sampling and monitoring are required to be maintained in accordance with 10 CFR 20.2103.
Information Notice 85-92, 'Surveys of Wastes Before Disposal from Nuclear Reactor Facilities,' is to be used as technical guidance.
All survey, sampling and monitoring instruments must have current calibrations appropriate for the types and energies of radiation to be detected.
ANSI N323, 'Radiation Protection Instrumentation Test and Calibration,' 1978, provides additional guidance in this area.
The licensee is required to keep exposure records (e.g., TLD or film badge).

steps.text: Review 50 percent of the facility's personnel dosimetry records.
Review the issue further to determine that the radiation exposure is consistent with the licensee's ALARA program.

Determine if the licensee reported the overexposures in accordance with 10 CFR Parts 20.2202 and 20.2203, how the licensee addressed the problem, and inform appropriate management and the responsible NRR project manager.
Review major changes in organization, personnel, facilities, equipment, programs, and procedures that could affect radiation protection activities since the last inspection.
Review any organizational change involving the radiation protection manager, including changes in authorities, responsibilities, position in the organizational structure, and reporting chains.

preconditions.text: Individuals routinely exceed 20 percent of the exposure limits.
There were exposures greater than the 10 CFR Part 20 limits.

constraints.text: The licensee is required to keep exposure records (e.g., TLD or film badge records) on form NRC-5 or equivalent (See 10 CFR 20.2106).
Changes in the facility or procedures described in the safety analysis report are required to be made in accordance with 10 CFR 50.59.

Be sensitive to changes that reduce the ability of the radiation protection manager to have direct recourse to the onsite facility manager in order to resolve concerns and issues related to the implementation of the radiation protection program.

steps.text: Review major radiation control events at the facility since the last inspection.
Verify if major incidents were reported to the NRC in accordance with 10 CFR 20.2202.
Verify if corrective actions were reviewed by the safety review committee and consistent with facility TS and procedures.
Inform appropriate management and the NRR project manager.
Verify ALARA implementation for one routinely performed experiment or process having an actual or potential for a relatively high radiological exposure.
Discuss ALARA considerations with operations or radiation protection personnel with respect to the selected activities.

preconditions.text: The inspection requirement has not already been adequately addressed during the assessment of personnel exposure records in the inspector's professional judgement.
Major incidents occurred.
Radiologically significant events were not effectively addressed by the licensee.

constraints.text: Ensure workers are aware of and understand the nature and cause of the events and the corrective actions taken.

steps.text: Determine the effectiveness of the licensee's ALARA program by conducting discussions with operations or radiation protection personnel.
Verify if facility supervisors periodically perform observations of staff effectiveness in radiological work practices, work monitoring, procedural compliance, and survey adequacy.
Verify if the radiation protection program is periodically reviewed and audits are performed by the radiation safety group.
Verify if the safety committee audits and evaluates the radiation protection program to assure compliance with 10 CFR Part 20, the TSs, and other program requirements.

preconditions.text: The ALARA program inspection was not previously covered and adequately evaluated under other inspection requirements.

constraints.text: Use respiratory protection equipment (and other controls) to limit intakes of radioactive material consistent with ALARA.
Declaration of pregnancy must be voluntary and must be in writing.
The declaration of pregnancy is revocable by the woman and the woman does not need to provide any 'medical proof' of pregnancy.
